meta.title: CSS Optimization – Reduce Load Time & Improve Performance (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to optimize CSS using minification, critical CSS, removing unused styles, and improving rendering performance.

content.value: CSS optimization is the process of improving how stylesheets are delivered and applied to reduce load time and improve rendering performance.
CSS can block rendering of a webpage. Optimizing CSS reduces render-blocking resources, speeds up page load, and improves user experience.
Critical CSS is the minimal CSS required to render above-the-fold content. It should be loaded first to improve perceived performance.
Unused CSS increases file size and slows down loading. Tools can help remove unused styles.

value: **Split CSS into smaller files if needed**

content.value: CSS optimization is essential for improving web performance. Proper techniques like minification, critical CSS, and removing unused styles ensure faster rendering and better user experience.

faq.question: What is critical CSS?
faq.answer: It is the CSS required to render above-the-fold content.

faq.question: Why minify CSS?
faq.answer: To reduce file size and improve load speed.

faq.question: How to remove unused CSS?
faq.answer: Use tools like PurgeCSS or build tools.

quiz.question: What is critical CSS used for?
